blocks.text: Section 3 Lecturers who wish to teach or are requested to teach in
the undergraduate program during midyear break shall be compensated on a fixed hourly rate based on the highest educational attainment and the expertise of the course(s)

Board Resolution No. 17-2005, Board taught pursuant to Resolution No. 44-2015, Board Resolution No. 55- and 2016. The hourly rate as embodied in the aforementioned resolutions shall be subject to change should there be amendments in the succeeding year(s).
VIII. Outside Teaching Load
Section 1 Outside teaching load refers to academic instruction
rendered in other colleges or universities. The schedule for outside teaching should be outside the regular official and overload teaching time.
Section 2 Faculty members who wish to teach outside should secure
the permission and approval from the program chair,
Associate Dean, Dean, Campus Director, Vice President for Academic Affairs, and the University President, respectively.
Section 3 An outside teaching load of not more than nine (9) units
for lecture and five (5) units for laboratory may be allowed to a faculty member.
Section 4 Faculty on study leave with pay or on scholarship shall not
be allowed to render outside teaching loads.
IX. Separability Clause
Section 1 If, for any reason, any part of provision of this policy and
guideline is declared invalid or unconstitutional, the remaining parts or provisions not affected thereby shall remain in full force and effect.
X. Effectivity
Section 1 These Policies on Faculty Workload approved by the
JRMSU Board of Regents per Resolution No. 145-2024
dated September 24, 2024 shall take effect this Academic

fixed_blocks.text:
20 Section 3 Lecturers who wish to teach or are requested to teach in the undergraduate program during midyear break shall be compensated on a fixed hourly rate based on the highest educational attainment and the expertise of the course(s). Board Resolution No. 17-2005, Board taught pursuant to Resolution No. 44-2015, Board Resolution No. 55- and 2016. The hourly rate as embodied in the aforementioned resolutions shall be subject to change should there be amendments in the succeeding year(s).

VIII. Outside Teaching Load

Section 1 Outside teaching load refers to academic instruction rendered in other colleges or universities. The schedule for outside teaching should be outside the regular official and overload teaching time.

Section 2 Faculty members who wish to teach outside should secure the permission and approval from the program chair, Associate Dean, Dean, Campus Director, Vice President for Academic Affairs, and the University President, respectively.

Section 3 An outside teaching load of not more than nine (9) units for lecture and five (5) units for laboratory may be allowed to a faculty member.

Section 4 Faculty on study leave with pay or on scholarship shall not be allowed to render outside teaching loads.

IX. Separability Clause

Section 1 If, for any reason, any part of provision of this policy and guideline is declared invalid or unconstitutional, the remaining parts or provisions not affected thereby shall remain in full force and effect.

X. Effectivity

Section 1 These Policies on Faculty Workload approved by the JRMSU Board of Regents per Resolution No. 145-2024 dated September 24, 2024 shall take effect this Academic Year 2024-2025.

qa_pairs.context:
20 Section 3 Lecturers who wish to teach or are requested to teach in the undergraduate program during midyear break shall be compensated on a fixed hourly rate based on the highest educational attainment and the expertise of the course(s). Board Resolution No. 17-2005, Board taught pursuant to Resolution No. 44-2015, Board Resolution No. 55- and 2016. The hourly rate as embodied in the aforementioned resolutions shall be subject to change should there be amendments in the succeeding year(s).

VIII. Outside Teaching Load

Section 1 Outside teaching load refers to academic instruction rendered in other colleges or universities. The schedule for outside teaching should be outside the regular official and overload teaching time.

Section 2 Faculty members who wish to teach outside should secure the permission and approval from the program chair, Associate Dean, Dean, Campus Director, Vice President for Academic Affairs, and the University President, respectively.

Section 3 An outside teaching load of not more than nine (9) units for lecture and five (5) units for laboratory may be allowed to a faculty member.

Section 4 Faculty on study leave with pay or on scholarship shall not be allowed to render outside teaching loads.

IX. Separability Clause

Section 1 If, for any reason, any part of provision of this policy and guideline is declared invalid or unconstitutional, the remaining parts or provisions not affected thereby shall remain in full force and effect.

X. Effectivity

Section 1 These Policies on Faculty Workload approved by the JRMSU Board of Regents per Resolution No. 145-2024 dated September 24, 2024 shall take effect this Academic Year 2024-2025.
qa_pairs.question: What is the maximum outside teaching load allowed for faculty members?
qa_pairs.answer: Not more than nine (9) units for lecture and five (5) units for laboratory.

qa_pairs.answer: The program chair, Associate Dean, Dean, Campus Director, Vice President for Academic Affairs, and the University President, respectively.

qa_pairs.question: What is the definition of program chair?
qa_pairs.answer: The program chair is the head of the department or program responsible for the administration of the academic programs.

qa_pairs.answer: No, they must be compensated based on the hourly rate embodied in the resolutions.

qa_pairs.context:
20 Section 3 Lecturers who wish to teach or are requested to teach in the undergraduate program during midyear break shall be compensated on a fixed hourly rate based on the highest educational attainment and the expertise of the course(s). Board Resolution No. 17-2005, Board taught pursuant to Resolution No. 44-2015, Board Resolution No. 55- and 2016. The hourly rate as embodied in the aforementioned resolutions shall be subject to change should there be amendments in the succeeding year(s).

VIII. Outside Teaching Load

Section 1 Outside teaching load refers to academic instruction rendered in other colleges or universities. The schedule for outside teaching should be outside the regular official and overload teaching time.

Section 2 Faculty members who wish to teach outside should secure the permission and approval from the program chair, Associate Dean, Dean, Campus Director, Vice President for Academic Affairs, and the University President, respectively.

Section 3 An outside teaching load of not more than nine (9) units for lecture and five (5) units for laboratory may be allowed to a faculty member.

Section 4 Faculty on study leave with pay or on scholarship shall not be allowed to render outside teaching loads.

IX. Separability Clause

Section 1 If, for any reason, any part of provision of this policy and guideline is declared invalid or unconstitutional, the remaining parts or provisions not affected thereby shall remain in full force and effect.

X. Effectivity

Section 1 These Policies on Faculty Workload approved by the JRMSU Board of Regents per Resolution No. 145-2024 dated September 24, 2024 shall take effect this Academic Year 2024-2025.
qa_pairs.question: What is the purpose of Section 2 of the separability clause?
qa_pairs.answer: Faculty members who wish to teach outside should secure the permission and approval from the program chair, Associate Dean, Dean, Campus Director, Vice President for Academic Affairs, and the University President, respectively.

qa_pairs.question: What is the definition of laboratory?
qa_pairs.answer: A place where scientific experiments or demonstrations are conducted.
